meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how strategic SaaS pricing and AI-driven workflows using Antigravity and Claude Code turn unfinished ideas into revenue.
content.answer_block: Abandonment stems from lack of market signal. By implementing usage-based pricing early, you force validation. Treat your SaaS as a financial engine, not a coding hobby. Use Antigravity to scaffold your infrastructure and Claude Code to iterate rapidly on pricing logic, ensuring your MVP generates revenue before you lose focus.
content.intro:
You have a graveyard of half-baked repositories and abandoned deployment pipelines. The silence of your analytics dashboard is deafening, not because the code is bad, but because the business case lacked the gravity of a real transaction. You aren't failing to code; you are failing to ship a product that demands payment.

Real founders don't wait for 'perfection'—they build for the transaction. By embedding your pricing model into the architecture from Day 1, you transform your technical debt into a commercial asset. It is time to stop building features and start building a business that pays you to finish.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Pricing Hook
steps.description: Identify the primary value metric (e.g., tokens, API calls, or seats) that scales with your user's success. This forces you to focus on the core feature that drives revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a pricing model specification document for a B2B SaaS. Define a tiered usage-based structure based on API request volume and user seats. Include a rationale for why this drives early-stage retention.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a pricing_model.md file outlining a tiered usage-based structure for a B2B SaaS application.'

steps.title: Architect the Metering Engine
steps.description: Don't build a billing system from scratch. Use an abstraction layer to track usage events. This ensures you can pivot your pricing without refactoring your entire database schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lightweight event-tracking service schema in TypeScript that logs usage events to a database, designed to be integrated with Stripe Metering.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a usage-tracking middleware that captures request volume and saves it to a PostgreSQL database via Prisma.'

steps.title: Automate Stripe Integration
steps.description: Connect your metering engine to Stripe. Using Claude Code to handle the boilerplate of webhooks ensures your billing logic is production-ready within minutes, not days.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust Stripe webhook handler in Node.js that listens for invoice.payment_succeeded and updates the user's subscription status in our database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement a secure Stripe webhook listener that validates signatures and updates subscription status in the DB.'

steps.title: Build the 'Pay-to-Play' Gate
steps.description: Implement a hard gate that checks usage limits before executing high-compute functions. This prevents resource exhaustion and forces users to upgrade.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a decorator function in TypeScript that checks if a user has exceeded their current subscription plan limits before allowing an API call to proceed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add a @checkUsageLimit decorator to my controller methods to enforce billing tiers.'

steps.title: Deploy the Revenue Loop
steps.description: Deploy your MVP to a staging environment and simulate a purchase flow. Testing the 'happy path' of a user paying for your product is the best cure for project abandonment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a testing script that simulates a user signing up, hitting their usage limit, and completing a Stripe checkout flow to upgrade their account.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create an end-to-end integration test for the subscription upgrade flow using Playwright.'

pro_tips: Never build a 'Free Forever' tier; it attracts users who will never pay and creates support debt.
Use 'Time-to-First-Dollar' as your primary KPI, not 'Time-to-First-Feature'.
If you cannot explain your pricing model in one sentence to a non-technical person, it is too complex.
Embed your billing logic as a dependency, not a side-effect, to ensure it is never bypassed.
Set up automated dunning emails immediately; recovering lost revenue is easier than acquiring new users.
content.next_steps: Once your billing loop is live, spend the next 48 hours getting one external user to pay for your MVP. If they won't pay, pivot the pricing or the feature set immediately.
content.conversion_block: You now have the infrastructure to turn code into cash. Don't let this momentum dissipate. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
